metadata_information.title: Economic Crises and Returns to University Education in Middle-Income Countries : Stylized Facts and COVID-19 Projections
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Economic Crises and Returns to University Education in Middle-Income Countries : Stylized Facts and COVID-19 Projections

document_description.abstract: This paper documents stylized facts on rates of returns to education during economic crises. It shows from three middle-income countries -- Indonesia, Pakistan, and South Africa -- that the rate of return to university education (versus secondary education) has increased during economic crises. Based on this stylized fact, the paper projects that the returns for university graduates may increase by at least one-quarter to one-third during the COVID-19 pandemic.
document_description.spatial_coverage: East Asia and Pacific ; South Asia ; Africa East

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
